title: IELTS Academic Reading April 2024 Passage 2 - Trauma Language in Popular Culture
rawPassageText:
READING PASSAGE 2
You should spend about 20 minutes on Questions 14-26, which are based on Reading Passage 2 below.

Trauma Language in Popular Culture

A

Only a generation ago, words such as trauma, trigger and dissociation were mostly heard in clinics, research papers or specialist training rooms. Today they appear in film reviews, song lyrics, celebrity interviews and short social media videos. A disappointing party may be described as traumatising, a tense scene in a television drama may need a trigger warning, and a character who avoids difficult memories may be said to be dissociating. This migration of clinical vocabulary into everyday speech is not simply a matter of fashion. It reflects a wider public interest in mental health and a new willingness to discuss experiences that were once hidden or dismissed.

B

The popular use of trauma language can be helpful because it gives people a vocabulary for suffering. Someone who has felt overwhelmed after a frightening event may find that a familiar term makes the experience easier to explain. Supporters argue that public language can reduce shame by showing that distress has causes and patterns, not just personal weakness. Yet the same vocabulary can also become imprecise. If every unpleasant memory is called trauma, the word may lose some of its clinical force, and people with severe symptoms may find their experiences treated as ordinary discomfort rather than serious harm.

C

Popular culture has been quick to adopt these words because they offer a simple way to organise a story. In films, novels and television series, a traumatic past can explain why a character is guarded, angry or unable to trust others. Used carefully, this can create sympathy and complexity. Used lazily, however, trauma becomes a shortcut: the audience is told that a character has a wound, and the wound is expected to do all the explanatory work. The risk is that trauma becomes a decorative backstory rather than a lived experience with social, physical and moral consequences.

D

Online platforms have accelerated this change. Short videos often translate complicated psychological ideas into memorable phrases, quizzes and personal checklists. A viewer may recognise a useful pattern and feel less isolated, but the format rewards certainty and speed. A behaviour that might have many explanations can be presented as definite evidence of trauma. Communities built around shared language can offer validation, but they can also make personal histories appear more uniform than they really are. The result is a culture in which psychological terms travel quickly, while the careful distinctions behind them often travel more slowly.

E

Researchers disagree about how worried the public should be. Dr Mira Patel, a clinical psychologist, argues that everyday trauma terms can act as a doorway to support when they help people describe distress that they would otherwise keep private. Professor Jonah Reed, a media scholar, is more cautious, warning that repeated use of clinical labels in entertainment may flatten important distinctions between discomfort, stress and traumatic injury. Dr Elena Morris, who studies digital communities, takes a middle position: online groups can provide useful validation, she says, but they may also spread oversimplified explanations when personal stories are treated as universal models.

F

For cultural critic Dr Theo Grant, the question is not whether trauma language should be banned from popular culture, but how audiences should read it. He suggests asking whether trauma is being used to deepen a character or merely to excuse weak writing. A serious representation usually shows causes, consequences and change over time; a weaker one uses a single revelation to solve the mystery of a person. As trauma vocabulary becomes part of ordinary culture, the challenge is to keep two truths in view: language can make pain speakable, but no popular term can replace careful listening.

Questions 14-19

Reading Passage 2 has six paragraphs, A-F.

Choose the correct heading for each paragraph from the list of headings below.

Write the correct number, i-viii, in boxes 14-19 on your answer sheet.

List of Headings

i The spread of clinical terms into ordinary culture

ii Benefits and risks of naming distress

iii When commercial storytelling makes trauma a shortcut

iv Why online formats speed up simplified explanations

v Different expert views on public trauma vocabulary

vi How audiences can judge trauma-based stories

vii The medical discovery of post-traumatic stress

viii A decline in public interest in mental health

14 Paragraph C
15 Paragraph B
16 Paragraph A
17 Paragraph D
18 Paragraph E
19 Paragraph F

Questions 20-23

Complete the summary below.

Choose ONE WORD ONLY from the passage for each answer.

Write your answers in boxes 20-23 on your answer sheet.

Trauma vocabulary in public life

The movement of trauma vocabulary into everyday speech shows a greater public interest in 20 .................... . Such language may reduce 21 .................... because it presents distress as something with causes and patterns. However, if the word trauma is applied too widely, it can become 22 .................... . On social media, psychological ideas are often turned into quizzes and personal 23 .................... .

Questions 24-26

Look at the following statements and the list of researchers below.

Match each statement with the correct researcher, A-D.

Write the correct letter, A-D, in boxes 24-26 on your answer sheet.

List of Researchers

A Dr Mira Patel
B Professor Jonah Reed
C Dr Elena Morris
D Dr Theo Grant

24 Common psychological labels

25 Medical terms used in popular media

26 Online groups
rawAnswerKey:
Question 14: Paragraph C

Correct Answer: iii

Accepted Answers: iii

Answer Group: IELTS Academic Reading April 2024 Passage 2 - Trauma Language in Popular Culture

Exact Portion: Used lazily, however, trauma becomes a shortcut

Short Thai Explanation: คำตอบคือ iii เพราะย่อหน้า C เน้นว่าหนัง นิยาย และซีรีส์ใช้ trauma เพื่อจัดระเบียบเรื่องราว บางครั้งใช้ดี แต่บางครั้งใช้แบบ lazy จนกลายเป็น shortcut หรือ backstory ตกแต่ง ไม่ใช่ประสบการณ์จริงที่ซับซ้อน

Paraphrased Vocabulary: storytelling = films, novels and television series; shortcut = trauma becomes a shortcut; commercial/popular culture = popular culture

Question 15: Paragraph B

Correct Answer: ii

Accepted Answers: ii

Answer Group: IELTS Academic Reading April 2024 Passage 2 - Trauma Language in Popular Culture

Exact Portion: The popular use of trauma language can be helpful because it gives people a vocabulary for suffering.

Short Thai Explanation: คำตอบคือ ii เพราะย่อหน้า B พูดทั้งข้อดีและความเสี่ยงของการตั้งชื่อความทุกข์ด้วย trauma language ข้อดีคือช่วยอธิบาย suffering และลด shame ส่วนข้อเสียคือคำอาจ imprecise และเสีย clinical force

Paraphrased Vocabulary: benefits = helpful / reduce shame; risks = imprecise / lose clinical force; naming distress = vocabulary for suffering

Question 16: Paragraph A

Correct Answer: i

Accepted Answers: i

Answer Group: IELTS Academic Reading April 2024 Passage 2 - Trauma Language in Popular Culture

Exact Portion: This migration of clinical vocabulary into everyday speech is not simply a matter of fashion.

Short Thai Explanation: คำตอบคือ i เพราะย่อหน้า A อธิบายว่าคำศัพท์ clinical เช่น trauma, trigger, dissociation ย้ายจากบริบทคลินิกไปสู่ everyday speech และ popular culture จึงตรงกับ heading เรื่องการแพร่ของ clinical terms เข้าสู่ภาษาทั่วไป

Paraphrased Vocabulary: spread = migration; clinical terms = clinical vocabulary; ordinary culture = everyday speech / popular culture

Question 17: Paragraph D

Correct Answer: iv

Accepted Answers: iv

Answer Group: IELTS Academic Reading April 2024 Passage 2 - Trauma Language in Popular Culture

Exact Portion: Online platforms have accelerated this change.

Short Thai Explanation: คำตอบคือ iv เพราะย่อหน้า D อธิบายว่า online platforms และ short videos ทำให้ psychological ideas เดินทางเร็วในรูปแบบ phrases, quizzes และ checklists แต่ความซับซ้อนถูกทำให้เรียบง่ายเกินไป จึงตรงกับ heading เรื่อง online formats และ simplified explanations

Paraphrased Vocabulary: speed up = accelerated / travel quickly; online formats = short videos / online platforms; simplified explanations = rewards certainty and speed

Question 18: Paragraph E

Correct Answer: v

Accepted Answers: v

Answer Group: IELTS Academic Reading April 2024 Passage 2 - Trauma Language in Popular Culture

Exact Portion: Researchers disagree about how worried the public should be.

Short Thai Explanation: คำตอบคือ v เพราะย่อหน้า E รวมมุมมองของนักวิจัยหลายคน ทั้ง Dr Mira Patel, Professor Jonah Reed และ Dr Elena Morris ซึ่งเห็นต่างกันเกี่ยวกับ trauma vocabulary ในสาธารณะ จึงเป็น different expert views

Paraphrased Vocabulary: expert views = researchers disagree; public trauma vocabulary = everyday trauma terms / clinical labels / online groups

Question 19: Paragraph F

Correct Answer: vi

Accepted Answers: vi

Answer Group: IELTS Academic Reading April 2024 Passage 2 - Trauma Language in Popular Culture

Exact Portion: He suggests asking whether trauma is being used to deepen a character or merely to excuse weak writing.

Short Thai Explanation: คำตอบคือ vi เพราะย่อหน้า F บอกวิธีที่ audience ควรอ่านหรือประเมินเรื่องเล่าที่ใช้ trauma language โดยถามว่า trauma ทำให้ตัวละครลึกขึ้นจริงไหม หรือแค่กลบ writing ที่อ่อน จึงตรงกับ heading เรื่อง how audiences can judge trauma-based stories

Paraphrased Vocabulary: audiences can judge = audiences should read / asking whether; trauma-based stories = trauma is being used to deepen a character

Question 20: Greater public interest, and movement ....................
Correct Answer: health

Accepted Answers: health

Answer Group: IELTS Academic Reading April 2024 Passage 2 - Trauma Language in Popular Culture

Exact Portion: It reflects a wider public interest in mental health

Short Thai Explanation: คำตอบคือ health เพราะ summary ใช้ถ้อยคำถอดความ ไม่ได้คัดลอกวลีจาก passage ตรงช่องว่าง — summary บอกว่า movement of trauma vocabulary into everyday speech แสดงถึง greater public interest in ____ และ passage ระบุว่า wider public interest in mental health ช่องว่างอยู่หลังคำว่า public interest in จึงเติม health เป็น ONE WORD ONLY จากคำว่า mental health


Paraphrased Vocabulary: greater public interest and movement = it reflects a wider public interest in mental health; answer signal = health

Question 21: Such wording can lower feelings of .................... because distress is framed as something with causes and remedies.
Correct Answer: shame

Accepted Answers: shame

Answer Group: IELTS Academic Reading April 2024 Passage 2 - Trauma Language in Popular Culture

Exact Portion: public language can reduce shame by showing that distress has causes and patterns

Short Thai Explanation: คำตอบคือ shame เพราะ summary ใช้ถ้อยคำถอดความ ไม่ได้คัดลอกวลีจาก passage ตรงช่องว่าง — summary แทบจะ paraphrase จาก passage ตรง ๆ ว่า language may reduce ____ because distress has causes and patterns ข้อความใน passage คือ public language can reduce shame by showing that distress has causes and patterns


Paraphrased Vocabulary: lower feelings = may reduce; framed as measurable = presents distress as something with causes; answer signal = shame

Question 22: Applied too widely, and can become ....................
Correct Answer: imprecise

Accepted Answers: imprecise

Answer Group: IELTS Academic Reading April 2024 Passage 2 - Trauma Language in Popular Culture

Exact Portion: Yet the same vocabulary can also become imprecise.

Short Thai Explanation: คำตอบคือ imprecise เพราะ summary ใช้ถ้อยคำถอดความ ไม่ได้คัดลอกวลีจาก passage ตรงช่องว่าง — summary พูดถึงกรณีที่ใช้คำว่า trauma กว้างเกินไป และ passage บอกว่า vocabulary can also become imprecise พร้อมยกตัวอย่างว่าถ้า every unpleasant memory is called trauma คำอาจเสีย clinical force


Paraphrased Vocabulary: too widely and can become = yet the same vocabulary can also become imprecise; answer signal = imprecise

Question 23: Social media, and turned into ....................
Correct Answer: checklists

Accepted Answers: checklists

Answer Group: IELTS Academic Reading April 2024 Passage 2 - Trauma Language in Popular Culture

Exact Portion: Short videos often translate complicated psychological ideas into memorable phrases, quizzes and personal checklists.

Short Thai Explanation: คำตอบคือ checklists เพราะ summary ใช้ถ้อยคำถอดความ ไม่ได้คัดลอกวลีจาก passage ตรงช่องว่าง — summary บอกว่า psychological ideas are often turned into quizzes and personal ____ ส่วน passage ระบุว่า quizzes and personal checklists คำตอบจึงเป็น checklists ซึ่งเป็นคำเดียวจาก passage


Paraphrased Vocabulary: social media and turned into = short videos often translate complicated psychological ideas into memorable phrases; answer signal = checklists

Question 24: Common psychological labels

Correct Answer: A

Accepted Answers: A

Answer Group: IELTS Academic Reading April 2024 Passage 2 - Trauma Language in Popular Culture

Exact Portion: Dr Mira Patel, a clinical psychologist, argues that everyday trauma terms can act as a doorway to support when they help people describe distress that they would otherwise keep private.

Short Thai Explanation: คำตอบคือ A เพราะ statement พูดว่า trauma terms ช่วยให้คนขอความช่วยเหลือได้ด้วยการมีภาษาอธิบาย distress ส่วน Dr Mira Patel บอกว่า everyday trauma terms เป็น doorway to support เมื่อช่วยให้คน describe distress ที่เดิมจะ keep private จึงตรงกับ A

Paraphrased Vocabulary: common psychological labels = mira patel clinical psychologist argues everyday trauma terms; answer signal = A



Question 25: Medical terms used in popular media

Correct Answer: B

Accepted Answers: B

Answer Group: IELTS Academic Reading April 2024 Passage 2 - Trauma Language in Popular Culture

Exact Portion: Professor Jonah Reed, a media scholar, is more cautious, warning that repeated use of clinical labels in entertainment may flatten important distinctions between discomfort, stress and traumatic injury.

Short Thai Explanation: คำตอบคือ B เพราะ Professor Jonah Reed เตือนว่า entertainment ใช้ clinical labels ซ้ำ ๆ อาจ flatten important distinctions ระหว่าง discomfort, stress และ traumatic injury ซึ่งตรงกับ statement ที่ว่า weaken the difference ระหว่าง stress กับ traumatic injury

Paraphrased Vocabulary: terms used popular media = professor jonah reed media scholar cautious warning repeated; answer signal = B



Question 26: Dr Morris argues that web-based networks can validate sufferers yet circulate reductive accounts

Correct Answer: C

Accepted Answers: C

Answer Group: IELTS Academic Reading April 2024 Passage 2 - Trauma Language in Popular Culture

Exact Portion: Dr Elena Morris, who studies digital communities, takes a middle position: online groups can provide useful validation, she says, but they may also spread oversimplified explanations

Short Thai Explanation: คำตอบคือ C เพราะ Dr Elena Morris พูดถึง online groups หรือ digital communities ว่าสามารถ provide useful validation แต่ก็อาจ spread oversimplified explanations ได้ ตรงกับ statement ว่า support people และ spread explanations that are too simple

Paraphrased Vocabulary: online groups = elena morris studies digital communities takes middle position; answer signal = C
